# Amplify

> Write with agents. Sound like yourself. A writing IDE for developers who write with AI agents.

Amplify is a free, open-source macOS writing IDE built around Claude Code. It gives your agent project-level context — a CLAUDE.md with voice rules and anti-patterns, a voice profile built from your past writing, and isolated workspaces per project — so every draft sounds like you.

## Key Facts

- **Platform:** macOS 14 Sonoma or later (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- **Price:** Free and open source (Apache 2.0)
- **No API key required:** Uses Claude Code, which you already have
- **All files are local:** Plain Markdown on your Mac. No sync, no database, no proprietary format
- **Project-level context:** Each workspace has a CLAUDE.md that gives the agent your voice rules, folder conventions, and writing commands on every interaction
- **Voice DNA:** Run `create voice dna` to analyze your past writing and generate a voice profile (tone, vocabulary, rhythm) that the agent uses automatically
- **Workspace isolation:** Each writing project is a separate folder with its own context. Your newsletter doesn't bleed into your technical blog

## Why not a note-taking app?

Note-taking apps send individual files to AI with no project context. Every prompt starts from scratch — no voice rules, no anti-patterns, no folder conventions. Amplify gives the agent a full project root with instructions (CLAUDE.md), a voice profile (voice-dna.md), and workspace conventions every time.

## Core Commands (via Claude Code terminal)

- `create voice dna` — Analyzes reference writing, generates `voice-dna.md` capturing tone, vocabulary, rhythm
- `brainstorm [topic]` — 10 content angles with working titles and hooks, saved to `ideas/`
- `draft [file]` — Full first draft in the author's voice, saved to `drafts/`
- `edit [file]` — Tightens a draft for clarity, voice consistency, and a 24-point humanizer checklist
- `critique [file]` — Honest structural and voice feedback without rewriting
- `replicate [file]` — Platform-specific versions (X thread, LinkedIn, Substack)
- `create content strategy` — Structured content strategy document

## Workspace Structure

```
workspace/
  ideas/          # Raw ideas, one Markdown file per idea
  drafts/         # Work-in-progress pieces
  published/      # Completed pieces with publish date
  references/     # Voice samples, style guides, swipe files
  voice-dna.md    # Author's voice profile (generated)
  CLAUDE.md       # Agent operating instructions (auto-generated)
  .writinghub/    # Internal config
```
